plan_summary: A 10-year diversification-resilience plan for Ravi Kumar's 5-acre farm in Kethepally, Suryapet. The plan retains the existing cotton (2 ac) and pigeonpea (1 ac) as short-term cash anchors, then progressively converts 2 acres into high-value perennials — Thailand Lemon (1 ac) and Dragon Fruit (0.5 ac pilot) — with commercial Moringa on the boundary. A Fuerte/Pollock Avocado micro-pilot (0.25 ac) is introduced in Year 2 after lemon establishment is confirmed. Dairy is upgraded from 1 local cross to 2 Sahiwal cows, and a 10-box Apis mellifera apiary is added for pollination synergy and honey income. ZBNF transition begins in Year 1 via Jeevamruta and vermicomposting, targeting organic certification by Year 4. Total Year 1 investment is kept within ₹4 lakh. By Year 6+, projected net farm income is ₹6–10 lakh/year across all enterprises.
farmer_profile_inferred: Small-scale Suryapet farmer, 5 acres split across two parcels of red chalka + red loam soil, pH 6.8 (ideal for perennials), drip-already-installed, bore well, 3 adult workers, moderate investment capacity (₹4L), wildlife pressure from monkeys + peacocks (3.5 km forest), prior maize loss to monkeys — avoid maize/open-grain crops. Transitioning to organic. Open to agroforestry. Existing cotton + pigeonpea provide near-term cash. Single local cross cow provides manure base. No storage or processing. Primary income: farming only. Risk tolerance: moderate with pilot-first preference.

soil_requirements: Red loam preferred; avoid waterlogged patches
crops.seasonal_price_windows: Oct–Dec harvest; prices peak Jan–Mar when arrivals thin
risk_flags: Price volatility is primary risk — hedge with PMFBY
Reduce to 1.5 ac by Year 4 as perennials mature
why_it_fits: Already established on farm — zero replanting cost
Red loam parcel suits cotton well
Existing KCC credit covers input costs
Provides Year 1–3 cash flow while perennials establish
value_addition_options: Sell to ginning mill for lint premium

climate_concerns: Excess moisture in black soil patches causes wilt — red soil parcel preferred

risk_flags: Sterility mosaic virus can wipe out crop — use certified virus-free seed
why_it_fits: Already thriving on farm — zero replanting cost
Fixes atmospheric nitrogen — reduces fertilizer cost for adjacent crops
Drought-tolerant — survives 2019/2023-type dry spells rainfed
MSP-backed price floor provides income security
Intercrop with lemon in Years 1–3 while trees establish

risk_flags: HLB disease — buy only certified disease-free grafts from SKLTSHU
Do NOT plant in waterlogged micro-patches

Drip already installed — critical for commercial lemon
Telangana structural deficit of 30–40K tonnes/year = ₹5–15/kg structural premium
Wildlife-resilient — monkeys and peacocks avoid mature citrus
Intercrop with pigeonpea in Years 1–3 for nitrogen and cash flow
40–50% price premium over Kagzi lime

Monsoon waterlogging — raised bed planting mandatory

Concrete pole + wire trellis setup is the largest cost — get 3 contractor quotes
Night-blooming flowers may need hand pollination for maximum fruit set

Minimal water requirement (~250mm equivalent) — suits bore well constraint
CRITICAL: Monkeys and wild boar avoid cactus — zero wildlife pressure
Peacocks do not damage cactus plants
Highest net ₹/acre of any crop in this plan at peak

crops.expected_yield_per_acre: Fresh pods: 8–12 tonnes/acre; Leaf powder: 1–2 tonnes/acre

disease_risks: Root rot if waterlogged — plant on bunds/boundaries with good drainage

Leaf powder processing requires drying shed (future investment Year 3+)

Leaf powder has export market (Hyderabad processors)
Nitrogen-fixing root zone improves adjacent soil

Hot dry westerly winds — moringa/drumstick windbreak on western boundary is essential
soil_requirements: Red loam parcel preferred (better water retention than chalka)
Raised beds 30–45cm if any drainage doubt
disease_risks: Phytophthora root rot — CRITICAL; use Ashdot 17 rootstock; avoid any waterlogging
Anthracnose post-harvest — copper sprays from fruit set

risk_flags: DO NOT use random-seedling grafts — Ashdot 17 rootstock only for Phytophthora resistance
DO NOT plant Hass variety — will fail in Suryapet summer
Pilot 0.25 acre strictly — expand only after Year 4 survival confirmation
Plant in Year 2 (not Year 1) — establish lemon first, learn drip management

₹150/kg current wholesale = highest ₹/kg of any fruit in this plan

Hyderabad metro demand for avocado growing 25–30%/year

integration_with_crops: 20–25 kg manure/cow/day → free Jeevamruta input for ZBNF transition
Manure covers ~2 acres of fertilizer need — reduces input cost ₹15,000–₹25,000/year
Urine collected for Jeevamruta preparation (ZBNF core input)
Biogas plant (future Year 2) converts manure to cooking gas + slurry compost

apiary.placement_strategy: Place 5 boxes within 100m of Thailand Lemon block (pollination boost 20–40% yield increase). Place remaining 5 boxes near Dragon Fruit + Moringa boundary. Relocate 3–4 boxes to cotton field during flowering (Oct–Nov) for additional pollination. Shade boxes from direct afternoon sun. Ensure water source within 50m.
apiary.expected_revenue_inr_per_year: ₹62,500–₹75,000 (honey) + ₹30,000–₹60,000 estimated pollination yield uplift on lemon + dragon fruit
pollination_benefit_to_crops: Thailand Lemon: 20–40% yield boost estimated ₹15,000–₹40,000/year additional revenue
Dragon Fruit: significant pollination benefit for fruit set
Moringa: bee-pollinated flowers improve pod set
